meta.positioning: The insight that frozen embeddings leak scanner/site is published prior art. NeuroAD Discovery Engine's contribution is productization: a runnable, agent-orchestrated referee that chains the full adversarial gauntlet, issues a fragile/robust verdict a named scientist can run in one command, gates survivors behind a plasma-biomarker anchor, and routes them to ONE falsifiable next experiment — with Claude as the adjudicator, not just the coder. It is a referee/auditor/red-team, NOT a co-scientist or discovery platform.

prior_art.title: Batch Effects in Brain Foundation Model Embeddings
prior_art.note: Tao et al. show brain-FM embeddings (BrainLM, SwiFT) encode substantial batch/site variability that often dominates diagnosis-related signal — the same 'star' mechanic. We cite it, we don't claim it. https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.14441

prior_art.title: Pretrained, Frozen, Still Leaking: Auditing Cross-Encoder Attribute Transfer in EEG Foundation Models
prior_art.note: Tai audits attribute leakage from FROZEN foundation-model embeddings with subject-disjoint lower bounds. Same-genre evidence (EEG modality) that frozen embeddings leak protected attributes. https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.09189

prior_art.title: Towards Robust Foundation Models for Digital Pathology (PathoROB)
prior_art.note: Digital-pathology robustness benchmark (PathoROB): biological vs non-biological variation across 34 medical centers. Same genre, different modality. https://www.nature.com/articles/s41467-026-73923-2

prior_art.title: REFUTE (Can Language Models Falsify?) / The AI Scientist-v2
prior_art.note: Automated scientific-claim falsification is an established sub-genre; our novelty is the closed AD-specific loop, not falsification per se.

SURVIVOR.biology_hypothesis: Survivor “AD vs CN diagnosis is decodable from OASIS structural-derived features.” routes to a amyloid-cascade (tau-driven) mechanism, keyed on p-tau217 and amyloid positivity. Plasma p-tau217: among the strongest blood AD biomarkers. AD-vs-CU AUC ~0.93-0.98; MCI-vs-CU ~0.94. Correlation with a structural probe score is MODEST (r~0.3-0.55), not redundant. SUPPORTED. The structural probe is read as a downstream imaging correlate of that molecular process, not an independent discovery.
next_experiment: Target N: ~120 complete-case subjects with the routing biomarker.
Direction: the structural probe score should track plasma p-tau217 (expected r~0.3-0.55, modest not redundant) and be enriched in amyloid-positive subjects.
Kill criterion: if the probe score shows no p-tau217 correlation (r<0.2) on the complete-case subset, the amyloid-cascade routing is wrong.
falsification: if the probe score shows no p-tau217 correlation (r<0.2) on the complete-case subset, the amyloid-cascade routing is wrong.
If the probe score's biomarker correlation disappears after residualizing against a scanner-predicting direction, the survivor was leakage after all.
If a held-out cohort fails to reproduce the effect (drop below the promotion floor), the mechanism claim is withdrawn.
caveats: Corroborated by REAL cross-cohort replication (held-out cohort), not a molecular marker: no open dataset pairs MRI with plasma p-tau217/GFAP. Replication is genuine independent evidence but weaker than a molecular anchor — a plasma-anchored confirmation needs gated data.
Completeness: 1/5 gauntlet tests could not run; score is renormalized over the tests that did.
Verdict is provisional and conditional on the scanner/site distribution of any replication cohort.
Brain-age adjustment uses a proxy control; residual aging signal cannot be excluded.
Any biomarker anchor is complete-case only and may not generalize.
Leakage is bounded, not eliminated — see the published batch-effect audits (‘Batch Effects in Brain Foundation Model Embeddings,’ arXiv:2604.14441 (2026)).
Promotion authorizes one confirmatory experiment, not adoption of the finding.
A ‘robust enough for follow-up’ verdict is a licence to follow up, not to conclude — the referee promotes hypotheses, it does not confirm them.

The brain-age control is a proxy, not a gold standard: it is embedding-derived (this cohort's fit: R2=0.57, MAE=6.3yr), so residual generic-aging signal can survive the adjustment and masquerade as disease-specific.
There is no usable plasma p-tau217 / GFAP anchor here, so the molecular gate is unmet — the finding rests on imaging alone.
Confirm the effective sample size per split — subject-disjoint cross-validation on a modest cohort leaves each fold thin.

The leakage test audits the embeddings with the same linear-probe family it uses for the outcome, so it bounds — but cannot fully rule out — shared confounding; this is the mechanic quantified in ‘Pretrained, Frozen, Still Leaking: Auditing Cross-Encoder Attribute Transfer in EEG Foundation Models’ (arXiv:2606.09189 (2026)), which we cite rather than claim.

courtroom.prosecution: The claim that OASIS structural-derived features (weight-free feeder; nWBV/eTIV/ASF) separate AD (CDR>=1) from CN (CDR=0) does not survive scrutiny. Even granting a +0.25 AUC leakage margin, that headroom is thin enough that acquisition confounds could account for much of the apparent effect. No single dimension cleanly collapses, but absence of a knock-out is not the same as demonstrated biology. The parsimonious reading is an acquisition/aging artifact until an orthogonal molecular anchor says otherwise.
courtroom.defense: The signal separating AD (CDR>=1) from CN (CDR=0) is consistent with real disease biology. It withstood: Age / sex adjustment, Site / scanner leakage, Brain-age control, Replication split. The outcome exceeds the scanner-prediction floor by +0.25 AUC on subject-disjoint splits — the effect is not merely which machine acquired the scan. The molecular anchor is incomplete, so the defense rests on the imaging survivors alone and asks only for follow-up, not acceptance.
courtroom.judge_reasoning: Robustness score 84/100 places this finding at 'robust enough for follow-up'. 1 dimension(s) could not be run and were excluded from the denominator — the verdict reflects only the evidence actually gathered. The +0.25 AUC leakage margin clears the acquisition confound, which is why the defense retains standing. The finding provisionally meets the promotion floor and may proceed to the biomarker-gated biology step — as a hypothesis to test, not a conclusion to bank.

SURVIVOR.narration: The claim “AD vs CN diagnosis is decodable from OASIS structural-derived features.” lands at “robust enough for follow-up” (84/100). The naive effect (AUC 0.82 separating AD (CDR>=1) from CN (CDR=0)) is real on paper, but the referee's job is to ask what else could produce it. It cleared the gauntlet's promotion floor: the effect exceeds the scanner-prediction floor on subject-disjoint splits and is worth a confirmatory follow-up — as a hypothesis, not a settled result. Where it stops being true: that a molecular anchor exists at all — with no p-tau217 / GFAP correlation measured here, the finding rests on imaging alone and would not survive a demand for orthogonal pathology. That the same frozen embeddings can predict scanner/site as well as outcome is documented prior art (“Batch Effects in Brain Foundation Model Embeddings,” arXiv:2604.14441 (2026)); NeuroAD Discovery Engine only runs the audit and issues the verdict.

cross_attention_fusion.disclaimer: FROM-SCRATCH numpy multi-head cross-attention used as a FIXED, non-trained FEATURE transformer: the Q/K/V projections and modality tokenizers are seeded random projections, so the cross-attention weights are data-dependent but nothing in the attention block is learned. The ONLY fitted component is the downstream logistic head, whose AUC is leakage-free (StandardScaler + PCA-10 + logistic fit inside each site-disjoint CV fold, bootstrap 95% CI, within-site permutation null via probe.auc_ci_perm). This is NOT the published vkola-lab/ncomms2025 ADRD transformer (that needs torch + GPU + gated weights) and is NOT a trained end-to-end model. ADNI-only decision support; NOT outcome-validated against known AD drugs.

courtroom.prosecution: The claim that ADNI z-standardized FreeSurfer morphometry (UCSFFSX7 ST regions; weight-free feeder) separate AD from CN does not survive scrutiny. The subject-disjoint leakage margin is only -0.05 AUC — the same head predicts the scanner nearly as well as the outcome, exactly the batch-effect mechanic reported in 'Batch Effects in Brain Foundation Model Embeddings' (arXiv:2604.14441 (2026)). It failed or weakened under: Site / scanner leakage (failed). The parsimonious reading is an acquisition/aging artifact until an orthogonal molecular anchor says otherwise.
courtroom.defense: The signal separating AD from CN is consistent with real disease biology. It withstood: Age / sex adjustment, Brain-age control, Biomarker anchor, Replication split. Critically, it is anchored to a plasma p-tau217 / GFAP correlation on the complete subset (r=0.49) — molecular pathology, not pixels.
courtroom.judge_reasoning: Robustness score 39/100 places this finding at 'fragile'. The -0.05 AUC leakage margin is the deciding fact: the prosecution's artifact reading is the more defensible one. The finding sits below the promotion floor; it is not carried forward to a mechanism, and would need a stronger leakage margin or molecular anchor to be worth a scientist's quarter.

KILL.narration: The claim “AD vs CN diagnosis is decodable from the full ADNI cohort (3T and 1.5T combined).” lands at “fragile” (39/100). The naive effect (AUC 0.94 separating AD from CN) is real on paper, but the referee's job is to ask what else could produce it. It sits below the promotion floor, so the honest read is that acquisition, demographics or generic aging can plausibly account for it, and it should not consume a scientist's quarter without stronger evidence. Where it stops being true: that the scanner/site mix in any new cohort matches this one — the leakage margin is only -0.05 AUC, so a different scanner distribution could erase the effect. That the same frozen embeddings can predict scanner/site as well as outcome is documented prior art (“Batch Effects in Brain Foundation Model Embeddings,” arXiv:2604.14441 (2026)); NeuroAD Discovery Engine only runs the audit and issues the verdict.

ad_signal.message: Frozen Neuro-JEPA embeddings separate clinical AD (CDR>=1) from CN at AUC 0.811 on real OASIS-1 volumes — the disease signal is carried by the model's own representation (matches structural ~0.82).
